location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 3+ years in product management or equivalent
Base pay range $128,000–$160,000 in NYC or SF, consistent with mid-to-senior IC
Expected to own products end-to-end and communicate with executive levels

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 3+ years in product management, product design or equivalent consultant experience with significant time spent leading consumer facing products
You manage project ambiguity, complexity, and interdependencies in an organized and structured way, and you are able to define and analyze metrics that inform the success of your products
Strong user empathy and experience in the customer discovery and product development process

Experience building alignment and influencing decision making across org lines and with senior leadership; specifically showcasing strong judgment when balancing a flawless user experience and the needs of a two sided marketplace
Skills and intuition for communication strategy at every level -- within teams, within the company, to users, and other audiences
Balancing multiple projects, deadlines, and requests should be second nature to you

scope.surface: Driver Experience platform — reducing friction, streamlining key interactions, and building intuitive/fair systems for drivers
core_responsibilities: Deeply understand driver and business needs to set an ambitious product vision that motivates and inspires your team, peers and leadership
Set your team's goals and success metrics that align with Lyft's mission and drive maximum impact based on data analysis, market research and user research
Build a product roadmap to achieve your goals and lead a core team of engineers, designers, data scientists, and marketers to execute on it.
Build alignment and collaborate with partner teams on shared goals and initiatives
Communicate roadmaps, priorities, experiments and decisions across a broad set of audiences from partner teams to executive levels
Completely own your team's products, existing or new, from ideation, through development, to launch, growth and maintenance

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned but not detailed

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($128K–$160K base in NYC/SF)
Clear product surface and ownership (Driver Experience)
Equity and bonus upside mentioned in addition to base
Generous benefits package including 18 weeks parental leave and mental health benefits
Hybrid flexibility including up to 4 weeks per year work-from-anywhere
red_flags: Hybrid role requires 3 specific days in office (Mon, Wed, Thu) — limited schedule flexibility
Salary range applies to NYC or SF only; other location pay not disclosed
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned at all
No mention of leveling (PM I, PM II, Senior PM) making it harder to gauge career stage fit

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years requirement and comp range)
scope.business_model (inferred from Lyft's rideshare product and 'two sided marketplace' mention)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated explicitly)
ambiguities: The JD mentions '3+ years in product management, product design or equivalent consultant experience' — product design and consulting are listed as equivalent paths, which broadens the candidate pool beyond pure PM backgrounds. Treated as a single hard requirement.
Compensation range is stated only for NYC and SF; roles in other locations may have different ranges per the JD, but no other ranges are given.
